_comment: Pre-flight plan for uploading the curated ONNX artifacts under E:/Models to the
huggingface.co/Heliosoph organization. Each entry pairs a local folder with its
target HF repo, license obligations, and provenance.
1. Check the local folder has the expected files (see `expectedContents`).
2. Copy the matching template from models/upload-readmes/<repoSuffix>.md into
the local folder as README.md. Fill in any [BRACKETED] placeholders.
3. Drop the upstream LICENSE file(s) listed in `licenseFiles` into the folder.
6. Once uploaded, copy the commit sha from the HF web UI into models/catalog.json's
License confidence: 'high' = stated explicitly on the upstream HF model card.
'medium' = inferred from related models / org defaults; double-check.
All license assignments below need a human eyeball before upload.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)
provenance.precision: fp32 (also produced as fp16 by passing -Fp16)

license.notes: Inherits CreativeML OpenRAIL-M from base SD 1.5 + Lykon's AbsoluteReality terms. Hyper-SD LoRA is OpenRAIL-M (ByteDance). Preserve both.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)
optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)

license.notes: Verify the model card on HF — epiCRealism's terms are inherited from SD 1.5 but the upstream repo sometimes lacks an explicit LICENSE file.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)

license.notes: Nitrosocke models predate the OpenRAIL-M standard but are typically tagged as such. Verify.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)
provenance.schedulerHint: Euler, 4 steps, CFG=1; activator token 'mdjrny-v4 style'

provenance.vae: stabilityai/sd-vae-ft-mse (paired in during export — RV6 ships without VAE)
provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)

license.notes: Realistic Vision is more NSFW-permissive than the other Hyper variants. Consider noting that on the model card.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum 1.24 + diffusers 0.31 + torch 2.4 (cu124)
provenance.precision: fp32 — the optimum 1.24 / torch 2.4 / opset-14 fp16 path produces a numerically broken UNet for SDXL-class models. Revisit fp16 when a confirmed working toolchain exists.

license.notes: Inherits SDXL CreativeML OpenRAIL++-M from stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0.

uploads.exportedBy: manual optimum-cli export (Florence-2 uses custom modeling code; not in export-batch-onnx.ps1)

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum-cli export onnx --task image-to-text --dtype fp16
provenance.notes: Florence-2 ships as 4 separate ONNX files (vision_encoder / encoder / decoder / embed_tokens). All four must be present at load time.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum-cli export onnx + post-export dynamic INT8 quantization
provenance.notes: Roughly half the disk + RAM of the fp16 variant; modest quality loss on text-heavy OCR prompts. Use this on CPU / mobile / NPU targets.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum-cli export onnx --task image-to-text

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded from upstream Kokoro release; not converted locally
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder before upload: copy kokoro-v1.0.onnx + the entire kokoro-voices/ subfolder under one parent, then upload that parent.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX is the upstream's primary distribution format
provenance.notes: Voice bin files (af.bin, am_michael.bin, bf_emma.bin, etc.) are required at inference. 11 voices total: 6 American (af_*, am_*) + 4 British (bf_*, bm_*) + the default `af`.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded from Megvii's release; not converted locally
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy the 7 yolox_*.onnx files into one folder, then upload. Bundled into one repo so users get the full size ladder in one download.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX checkpoints published by Megvii
provenance.notes: AGPL-free alternative to YOLOv8. Same 80-class COCO labels. Recommend yolox_s for general use, yolox_nano for CPU/edge, yolox_x for max accuracy.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Meta SAM mask decoders + ChaoningZhang/MobileSAM image encoder
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy the 3 SAM files into one folder, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: Meta SAM (mask decoders) + ChaoningZhang/MobileSAM (ViT-T image encoder)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — community ONNX exports
provenance.notes: MobileSAM ViT-T encoder (~27 MB) pairs with either SAM mask decoder variant (single-mask or multi-mask output). Together they form a complete SAM pipeline at ~60 MB total — orders of magnitude smaller than original SAM ViT-H.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Qin et al. U²-Net ONNX weights via danielgatis/rembg release
uploads.uploadStaging: Fetch both: https://github.com/danielgatis/rembg/releases/download/v0.0.0/u2net.onnx (~170 MB) and https://github.com/danielgatis/rembg/releases/download/v0.0.0/u2netp.onnx (~4.7 MB). Place both files + README.md + LICENSE into a single staging folder, then upload. Bundled into one repo so users get the small/large pair in a single download.

provenance.baseModel: xuebinqin/U-2-Net — Apache-2.0 academic release (Qin et al., Pattern Recognition 2020)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX checkpoints republished by danielgatis/rembg from the original U²-Net release
provenance.notes: Two-variant pair. Input: NCHW [1, 3, 320, 320] float32, RGB, normalized with mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406] / std=[0.229,0.224,0.225] (ImageNet). Output: 7 saliency maps at decreasing resolution (d0..d6); d0 is the final fused mask. Both files share the same input/output tensor signature so code can swap variants without changes. Trained on DUTS-TR (academic-public dataset). The separate u2net_portrait variant exists upstream but is explicitly excluded here — it inherits non-commercial restrictions from the APDrawing training data.

license.notes: Original Apache-2.0 from xuebinqin/U-2-Net is the authoritative license. The danielgatis/rembg release just bundles those weights — no relicensing. Excluding u2net_portrait keeps the bundle commercially clean (its APDrawing training data has non-commercial restrictions).

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Intel ISL DPT-Large monocular depth estimator
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy dpt_large_384.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — Intel-published ONNX
provenance.notes: Monocular depth estimation, 384×384 input, ~330M params. Outputs a single-channel depth map (relative depth, not metric).

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Intel ISL MiDaS v2.1 small (GitHub release v2_1)
uploads.uploadStaging: Fetch from https://github.com/isl-org/MiDaS/releases/download/v2_1/midas_v21_small_256.onnx . Place into a single staging folder with README.md + LICENSE, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: isl-org/MiDaS v2.1 small (EfficientNet-Lite3 encoder + lightweight depth decoder)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — checkpoint published directly in isl-org/MiDaS GitHub release v2_1
provenance.notes: Input: NCHW [1, 3, 256, 256] float32, **BGR** (not RGB — distinct from DPT-Large), normalized with ImageNet mean/std (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]). Output: [1, 256, 256] single-channel relative depth (higher = closer). ~21M params, ~50 ms / image on consumer CPU. Pair with DPT-Large in the same catalog as the small / large tradeoff for the depth task.

license.notes: isl-org/MiDaS GitHub repo is MIT. Distinct from Intel/dpt-large on HuggingFace which Intel re-released under Apache-2.0 — same family, different distribution path, different licenses. The v2_1 GitHub release inherits MIT from the upstream repo.

uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy ch_PP-OCRv4_det.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: PaddlePaddle PP-OCRv4 detection model (Chinese-tuned, also works on English)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — PaddlePaddle paddle2onnx export
provenance.notes: Text-region detector — input to an OCR recognizer (TrOCR / PP-OCRv4 recognizer / Florence-2 OCR). Outputs polygon coordinates for each detected text region. Despite the 'ch_' prefix, detection is largely language-agnostic — the script-specific weights live in the recognizer, not the detector.

license.notes: Baidu / Chinese-affiliated source — Heliosoph mirror is a trade-stability hedge.

uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy realesr-general-x4v3.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — Real-ESRGAN-published ONNX
provenance.notes: Tiny (~5 MB), 4× resolution upscale on general images (photos, illustrations, screenshots). The 'general-x4v3' variant is the latest generic-purpose model — separate anime / face variants exist upstream if needed later.

license.notes: Chinese-affiliated author — Heliosoph mirror is a trade-stability hedge.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Qualcomm AI Hub release of MediaPipe Face (Apache-2.0 ONNX via zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch)
uploads.uploadStaging: Two-step staging. (1) Download both zips: float = https://qaihub-public-assets.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/qai-hub-models/models/mediapipe_face/releases/v0.53.1/mediapipe_face-onnx-float.zip ; int8 = https://qaihub-public-assets.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/qai-hub-models/models/mediapipe_face/releases/v0.53.1/mediapipe_face-onnx-w8a8.zip . (2) Unzip each. Rename the extracted directories to float/ and int8/, place both inside a single staging folder. (3) Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging root. (4) Upload that staging folder.

provenance.baseModel: Google MediaPipe Face Detection (BlazeFace) + Face Landmark (6-point)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — Qualcomm AI Hub ONNX export via zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch PyTorch port
provenance.precision: float (fp32) + W8A8 (INT8 dynamic) — both shipped as subfolders
provenance.notes: Two-model pipeline: face_detector finds bounding boxes; face_landmark_detector returns 6 keypoints per face (eyes, nose tip, mouth, eye tragions). External weights pattern — each .onnx has a .data sibling that must travel with it. ~3 MB total per variant.

license.notes: License chain: Google MediaPipe (Apache-2.0) → zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch port (Apache-2.0) → Qualcomm AI Hub ONNX export (Apache-2.0). Google trained on commercial-friendly data; clean for any use.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — ONNX Model Zoo published checkpoint (opset 12) + curated ImageNet-1K class file
uploads.uploadStaging: Two-step staging. (1) Fetch the ONNX from https://github.com/onnx/models/raw/main/validated/vision/classification/mobilenet/model/mobilenetv2-12.onnx . (2) Drop a matching imagenet-classes.json (1000-entry array, index = argmax class, value = human-readable label — see imagenet_classes.txt / clsidx_to_labels mappings in PyTorch / Keras docs). (3) Place both files + README.md + LICENSE in one staging folder, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: Google MobileNetV2 — ImageNet-1K trained, 224x224 input, ~3.5M params
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX Model Zoo publishes the checkpoint
provenance.notes: Input: NCHW [1,3,224,224] float32, RGB, ImageNet-normalized (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]). Output: [1,1000] logits — apply softmax + argmax for top-1, then index into imagenet-classes.json. Use opset 12 file (mobilenetv2-12.onnx); opset 7 also exists upstream but lacks newer ORT optimizations.

license.notes: ONNX Model Zoo is Apache-2.0. The underlying MobileNetV2 architecture and weights originate from Google Research and are also Apache-2.0 (TensorFlow Slim release).

uploads.uploadStaging: Run scripts/export-swinir.ps1. The script clones JingyunLiang/SwinIR, downloads the two pinned .pth checkpoints from the v0.0 GitHub release, builds the SwinIR PyTorch model with the matching configs, and writes the two .onnx files into $env:DATUM_MODELS/swinir-staging/. Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging folder before upload.

provenance.exportToolchain: torch.onnx.export from upstream PyTorch checkpoints (v0.0 GitHub release). Variants: 003_realSR_BSRGAN_DFOWMFC_s64w8_SwinIR-L_x4_GAN.pth (real-world SR) + 005_colorDN_DFWB_s128w8_SwinIR-M_noise25.pth (color denoising σ=25).
provenance.notes: Two-variant bundle. swinir_realsr_x4.onnx: SwinIR-L, 240-dim, 9 RSTB blocks; 64x64 input -> 256x256 output (4× SR); RGB float32 [0,1] in/out. swinir_denoising_color_25.onnx: SwinIR-M, 180-dim, 6 RSTB blocks; 128x128 input -> 128x128 output; RGB float32 [0,1] in/out. Spatial dims fixed (window-shift in attention is brittle under dynamic axes for ORT); batch is dynamic. Consumers tile larger images at runtime.

license.notes: Apache-2.0 from the authoritative upstream (JingyunLiang/SwinIR). We re-export from upstream weights rather than mirror the personal wuminghao/swinir HF account — personal accounts can vanish or change without notice.

uploads.uploadStaging: Run scripts/export-kair.ps1 in a loop over all 8 .pth checkpoints in the local cszn/SCUNet repo's model_zoo/ folder (5 color variants with -Model scunet-color, 3 gray variants with -Model scunet-gray). The script writes each ONNX as an .onnx + .onnx.data sibling pair (torch externalizes weights at this opset/size). All 16 files land in $env:DATUM_MODELS/kair-staging/. Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging folder before upload — both files for every variant MUST travel together (the .onnx references the .data by relative filename).

provenance.baseModel: cszn/SCUNet — Apache-2.0 (Zhang et al., 'Practical Blind Image Denoising via Swin-Conv-UNet and Data Synthesis', 2022)
provenance.exportToolchain: torch.onnx.export from the 8 upstream PyTorch checkpoints. Color variants: SCUNet(in_nc=3, config=[4]*7, dim=64). Gray variants: SCUNet(in_nc=1, config=[4]*7, dim=64).
provenance.notes: Eight-variant bundle. Color (in_nc=3, 5 variants): scunet_color_{15,25,50} are Gaussian-noise specialists; scunet_color_real_{psnr,gan} are blind real-world denoisers. Gray (in_nc=1, 3 variants): scunet_gray_{15,25,50} are Gaussian specialists for grayscale workflows. Input: NCHW float32 in [0,1]; H and W must be divisible by 8 (internal downsampling). Output: same shape, denoised. Dynamic axes enabled for batch + H + W. Each variant is ~73 MB (3.8 MB .onnx + 70 MB .onnx.data external weights); the .onnx and .onnx.data files MUST be uploaded together.

uploads.exportedBy: Optimum ONNX export from vikhyatk/moondream2 PyTorch weights (manual / one-off; no reproducible script in the repo yet — flag for a future scripts/export-moondream2.ps1 if a clean re-export is ever needed)
uploads.uploadStaging: Copy the existing local Optimum export at $env:DATUM_MODELS/moondream2-onnx/ into a staging folder. Verify the file list matches expectedContents above (Optimum's exact output filenames depend on the optimum / transformers version used at export time — adjust the list to match what's actually there). Drop README.md + LICENSE into the staging root. The export may produce additional `.onnx.data` sidecars for any component whose weights exceed the protobuf 2GB limit — include those too. Upload the staging folder via huggingface-cli.

provenance.baseModel: vikhyatk/moondream2 — Apache-2.0 (Vikhyat Korrapati, 2024). 1.86B-param vision-language model: SigLIP vision encoder + Phi-1.5-based text decoder + cross-modal projection.
provenance.exportToolchain: Optimum ONNX exporter from upstream PyTorch weights. Multi-file export pattern: vision encoder, text decoder (with KV-cache merged), and embed_tokens as separate ONNX files. Original conversion was done locally; the exact toolchain version isn't recorded — re-derivation would need someone to re-run `optimum-cli export onnx --model vikhyatk/moondream2 --task image-to-text` against current Optimum + verify the output matches.
provenance.notes: Re-hosted under Heliosoph for distribution stability — vikhyatk/moondream2 doesn't ship its own ONNX exports, the file layout drifts across revisions, and runtimes that need ONNX have nowhere upstream to point at. This is the canonical ONNX form for the model. Drive via raw onnxruntime sessions (onnxruntime-genai's model_builder doesn't currently accept Moondream2's architecture).

license.notes: Apache-2.0 inherited from the authoritative upstream vikhyatk/moondream2 PyTorch weights. ONNX-export step doesn't change licensing — same model, different serialization.

uploads.uploadStaging: Run scripts/export-zoedepth.ps1 once for the fp32 model and once with -Fp16 for the fp16 sibling. Both runs write into $env:DATUM_MODELS/zoedepth-staging/ — the fp16 pass appends model_fp16.onnx alongside the fp32 model.onnx without overwriting. The script uses transformers' ZoeDepthForDepthEstimation class + a thin wrapper that unwraps the DepthEstimatorOutput dataclass into a single tensor so torch.onnx.export can trace cleanly (optimum-cli's onnx-export registry doesn't know about ZoeDepth in 1.24). Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging root before upload.

provenance.baseModel: Intel/zoedepth-nyu-kitti — MIT (Bhat et al., 'ZoeDepth: Zero-shot Transfer by Combining Relative and Metric Depth', 2023). DPT-Large backbone + dual metric-bin heads (NYU indoor + KITTI outdoor calibration).
provenance.exportToolchain: transformers 4.45.2 + ZoeDepthForDepthEstimation + a one-line Wrapper module that returns .predicted_depth as a bare tensor; torch.onnx.export with opset 17 + do_constant_folding=True + dynamic axes for batch/height/width. fp16 sibling produced via onnxconverter-common.float16 from the fp32 trace.
provenance.precision: fp32 (model.onnx) + fp16 (model_fp16.onnx) — same architecture, different precision.
provenance.notes: Input: NCHW [batch, 3, H, W] float32 RGB preprocessed by ZoeDepthImageProcessor (normalize, resize so H and W are divisible by 32). Output: [batch, H, W] float32 single-channel depth map in **meters** (real-world scale, not relative). Dynamic axes enabled — feed any 32-aligned spatial dims at inference. The fp32 model came in at 1.37 GB — just under the 2 GB protobuf limit, so no external-data sidecar. Both .onnx files are self-contained.

license.notes: MIT inherited from upstream Intel/zoedepth-nyu-kitti. The fp16 conversion is a numerical transformation only — no relicensing implication.

uploads.exportedBy: scripts/export-trocr-base-printed-fp16.ps1 (despite the -fp16 suffix on the script name, it now produces both fp32 + fp16 .onnx files in the same staging folder for the bundled Heliosoph repo)
uploads.uploadStaging: Run scripts/export-trocr-base-printed-fp16.ps1. It runs optimum-cli to produce fp32 ONNX into a staging dir, then onnxconverter-common.float16 (with keep_io_types=True) to cast to fp16, then copies BOTH precision .onnx files into the output's onnx/ subdir alongside the shared tokenizer + config files. Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging root before upload. Bundled into one Heliosoph repo (fp32 + fp16 sharing tokenizer / configs) for symmetry — the two catalog entries (trocr-base-printed + trocr-base-printed-fp16) differ only in their include glob.

provenance.baseModel: microsoft/trocr-base-printed — MIT (Li, Lv, Yu, Cui, Lu, Wei, 2021). Encoder-decoder VLM: ViT image encoder + GPT-style text decoder, trained on printed-text crops (SROIE dataset).
provenance.exportToolchain: Two-step pipeline: (1) optimum-cli `--task image-to-text-with-past` produces the fp32 encoder + merged decoder ONNX; (2) onnxconverter-common.float16 casts the fp32 graph in place to fp16 with keep_io_types=True (inputs/outputs stay fp32 at the wire boundary). Avoids optimum-cli's --dtype fp16 CUDA-only path + its If-subgraph wiring bug on merged decoders.
provenance.precision: fp32 (model.onnx pair) + fp16 (model_fp16.onnx pair) — same architecture, different precision. fp16 keeps fp32 IO types so the runtime feeds the same input tensors regardless of variant.
provenance.notes: Re-hosted under Heliosoph as a single bundled repo. Both fp32 and fp16 share the same tokenizer + config files at the repo root; per-precision .onnx files live under onnx/. Catalog entries pick precision via include glob (encoder_model.onnx vs encoder_model_fp16.onnx).

license.notes: MIT inherited from upstream microsoft/trocr-base-printed. Optimum ONNX export + fp16 conversion are numerical transformations only — no relicensing implication.

uploads.uploadStaging: Run scripts/export-triposr.ps1. The script clones VAST-AI-Research/TripoSR for the `tsr/` architecture module, downloads weights from stabilityai/TripoSR via the HF Hub, and traces two ONNX graphs: triplane.onnx (image→triplane features; weights spill to triplane.onnx_data sidecar via external_data_format) and nerf.onnx (triplane+xyz→density+color). Also writes a reproducibility quartet (requirements.txt / requirements-torch.txt / requirements-freeze.txt / README.txt) capturing the exact venv state. Drop a LICENSE file in the staging root before upload — upstream doesn't ship a LICENSE file (their model card just declares `license: mit` in YAML frontmatter), so write a standard MIT LICENSE text with copyright attributed to Stability AI + Tripo AI / VAST-AI-Research as the model authors. Bundle is ~3.4 GB on the wire — triplane.onnx_data dominates at ~3.1 GB; the .onnx graph file is ~900 KB.

provenance.baseModel: stabilityai/TripoSR (HF weights at config.yaml + model.ckpt) + VAST-AI-Research/TripoSR (GitHub architecture module). Single-image-to-mesh: DINOv1 image tokenizer + cross-attention triplane transformer + triplane post-processor + NeRF MLP. Tochilkin, Pankratz, Liu, Huang, Letts, Li, Liang, Laforte, Jampani, Cao (2024).
provenance.exportToolchain: torch.onnx.export from upstream PyTorch weights. Two-graph split: (1) triplane.onnx encapsulates image_tokenizer + tokenizer + backbone + post_processor (the per-image cost); (2) nerf.onnx encapsulates renderer.query_triplane + decoder (the per-chunk cost queried over a 3D point grid). opset 17 (grid_sample), do_constant_folding=True, external_data_format=True for the triplane graph.
provenance.precision: fp32 (default). Pass -Fp16 to the script for half-precision siblings triplane_fp16.onnx + triplane_fp16.onnx_data + nerf_fp16.onnx, ~half the disk footprint, IO types kept fp32.
provenance.notes: Re-hosted under Heliosoph for catalog distribution. Marching cubes is NOT part of either ONNX graph — that step lives in the host engine via the mesh_compute_* SQL function family. Standard convention for implicit-field mesh-gen models. The reproducibility quartet (requirements*.txt + README.txt) lets anyone with just these files rebuild the exact venv that produced them — pinned PyPI deps, torch + torchvision cu124 wheels, and the TripoSR architecture-repo commit sha at trace time.

license.notes: MIT per the upstream model card's YAML frontmatter (`license: mit`). The stabilityai/TripoSR HF repo is also gated via `extra_gated_fields` requiring a user to provide name/email/affiliation + opt-in to Stability marketing — that's HF's access-gate mechanism, NOT a license-binding click-through. The MIT license alone governs what users can do with the bytes once obtained. Heliosoph re-host: distribute under MIT freely; downstream users get standard MIT terms (no $1M threshold, no AUP propagation requirements). Upstream ships no LICENSE file in the repo, so the Heliosoph mirror should include a freshly-written one with proper MIT copyright attribution to Stability AI + Tripo AI.

licenseFiles.name: LICENSE
licenseFiles.notes: Use canonical MIT template — Stability didn't ship a LICENSE file at the upstream repo, just the `license: mit` declaration in the model card YAML. Author the LICENSE locally with `Copyright (c) 2024 Stability AI and Tripo AI / VAST-AI-Research` as the holders before upload.

rejected.reason: Ultralytics YOLOv8 is AGPL-3.0; redistributing under Heliosoph/ would impose AGPL obligations on downstream consumers. Use yolox-onnx bundle instead — same task, Apache-2.0.
Meta MusicGen is CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial only). Cannot mirror under Heliosoph/ without implying commercial use is OK. Catalog should reference Meta's repo directly if used at all.

Mirroring is legally permitted under the Stability AI Community License / OpenRAIL++-M, but they're high-traffic models that benefit more from being pointed at the upstream pre-converted ONNX (when available). Revisit if upstream ONNX is missing or stale.
All have first-party or onnx-community / Xenova mirrors on HF already. Catalog references those instead of mirroring.

InsightFace's umbrella policy restricts ALL released model weights to non-commercial research use, rooted in the WIDER FACE dataset's academic-only terms. The SCRFD source code is Apache-2.0 (OpenMMLab) but the trained weights are encumbered. Replaced with mediapipe-face-onnx (Google MediaPipe via Qualcomm, Apache-2.0, trained on commercial-friendly data).
